stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Llama-3 indicates relevance to the latest advancements in large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and activity around a specific, powerful open-source language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks, indicating its LLM nature.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device for privacy/control.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
stories.author: Justine Calma
bullet_points: Massive data centers are the physical infrastructure supporting the expansion of AI technologies.
The rapid build-out of these energy-intensive facilities is causing global conflicts.
Key concerns include strain on power grids, increased utility costs, community impact, and environmental concerns.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature by improving source citation.
This change aims to provide users with more transparent and verifiable information from search results.
The update represents a course correction in Google's AI search strategy regarding attribution.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both actor motion and camera trajectory.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while allowing per-frame control over intrinsic and extrinsic camera parameters.
The model leverages any pretrained image-to-video diffusion model conditioned on scene depth and camera information.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI Co-Mathematician' workbench designed to assist mathematicians in open-ended research.
The system leverages AI agents to support the entire mathematical workflow, from ideation and literature search to theorem proving.
Key features include an asynchronous, stateful workspace capable of managing uncertainty and tracking failed hypotheses.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and camera cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a novel scene while allowing per-frame control over intrinsic and extrinsic camera parameters.
The model is designed to be plug-and-play, building upon any pretrained image-to-video diffusion model that accepts scene depth conditioning.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: Current global LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback, are shown to be misleading.
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs indicates that the top models are statistically indistinguishable.
The study suggests that the current ranking methodology fails to accurately differentiate between high-performing LLMs.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered deep research agents face a critical vulnerability: their citations cannot be reliably verified.
Existing solutions (self-citation or standard RAG) fail to validate source accessibility, relevance, or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel source attribution evaluation framework using an AST parser to address this verification gap.

stories.title: CPanel's Black Week: 3 New Vulnerabilities Patched After Attack on 44k Servers
stories.author: ggallas
bullet_points: CPanel experienced a security incident resulting in attacks on approximately 44,000 servers.
The incident led to the patching of three newly discovered vulnerabilities.
This highlights ongoing security risks within widely used web hosting infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under the 'text-generation' pipeline, indicating a focus on generative AI.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, pointing to standard modern NLP infrastructure.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is the simplified setup, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local use on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux versions of image generation is planned for the near future.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-source models, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Amazon is adding a vertical video feed to Prime Video
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon Prime Video is integrating a vertical video feed, dubbed 'Clips', into its streaming service.
This move positions Prime Video to compete with short-form video trends popularized by platforms like TikTok and rivals like Netflix/Disney Plus.
The Clips feed allows users to sample short content and then transition to renting or purchasing the full movie or show.

stories.title: Here is Yarbo’s promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Robot mowers from Yarbo have been vulnerable to hacking, allowing attackers to steal sensitive user data.
The vulnerability allows hackers to access personal information such as GPS coordinates, Wi-Fi passwords, and emails.
Yarbo has issued a statement promising a fix for the security flaws in their robot mowers.

stories.title: The new Wild West of AI kids’ toys
stories.author: Sophie Charara, WIRED.com
bullet_points: AI-powered children's toys are emerging as a significant new market segment.
These connected companions have the potential to disrupt traditional forms of play and storytelling.
The rapid development of these toys is prompting regulatory scrutiny and calls for potential bans from lawmakers.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article discusses the technical challenge of integrating electronic manufacturing with flexible geometries.
The focus is on the difficulty of manufacturing mobile or movable qubits.
This points to ongoing hurdles in scaling and engineering advanced quantum computing hardware.

stories.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A cyberattack disrupted the widely used learning platform, Canvas.
The incident occurred during the critical period of year-end finals.
Schools and colleges nationwide were forced to postpone scheduled tests.

stories.title: Elon Musk faces criminal probe in France after ignoring summons in X case
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Elon Musk is under a criminal probe in France related to the X platform.
French authorities are threatening criminal charges if Musk fails to appear for questioning.
The story highlights regulatory and legal challenges facing major tech figures.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has a rigid, isolated expert set.
Suggests that replacing learned routing with uniform random routing in deeper layers causes minimal accuracy degradation (1.0-1.6 points).

stories.title: BAMI: Training-Free Bias Mitigation in GUI Grounding
stories.author: Jiwen Lu
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in GUI grounding, a key capability for GUI agents performing tasks like clicking and dragging.
The study identifies two primary sources of error in complex GUI scenarios: precision bias due to high image resolution and ambiguity bias from intricate interface elements.
A novel method, Masked Prediction Distribution (MPD) attribution, is proposed to achieve training-free bias mitigation.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
Introduces 'VHG,' a new method that uses verifiers to ensure the mathematical validity of generated problems.
Offers an alternative to expensive human expert involvement or flawed self-play methods in problem generation.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established ground-truth benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployment auditing.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user rather than an expert.
The proposed advancement aims to improve efficiency by incorporating strong priors, reducing latency and unnecessary retrieval rounds.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The study addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current research is criticized for fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are noted to be heavily skewed towards action recognition, overlooking broader real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents due to purely reactive current methods.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to explicitly incorporate trajectory-level strategy into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making processes.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a new architecture for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models.
Challenges the conventional design where each transformer layer has its own isolated expert set.
Suggests that replacing layer-specific routing with uniform random routing has a minimal impact on downstream accuracy.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs are good at solving problems but weak at generating novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
Existing problem generation methods are either too reliant on expensive human experts or suffer from invalid outputs via naive self-play.
The paper introduces VHG, a new approach that incorporates verification to generate high-quality hard problems for mathematical reasoning.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The paper investigates the role of optimizers in Large Language Model (LLM) training, specifically during finetuning.
A key finding is that using the same optimizer from the pretraining phase during full finetuning leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This approach reportedly outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during Supervised Finetuning (SFT).

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist for a specific domain.
Proposes a framework for 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' based on fixed scenario packs and defined auditing contracts.
Highlights the necessity of strict procedural controls (rubric, auditor, sampling configuration) to ensure the validity of safety assessments.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a black box, relying on iterative, exploratory querying.
This current method is likened to a newcomer searching a database, lacking the strong domain priors of an expert.
The proposed 'Superintelligent Retrieval Agent' aims to improve efficiency by moving beyond simple iterative searching to more expert-like navigation.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current MMDG research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic LLMs, which require the full model even for narrow tasks.
It investigates Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models as a solution to reduce computational load by activating only relevant experts.
The research highlights a practical limitation of current MoE implementations: restricting inference to a subset of experts often causes severe performance degradation.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a critical limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging mathematical problems.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to ensure generated problems are mathematically sound, overcoming issues with naive self-play.
The research aims to advance LLM training and facilitate autonomous scientific research by providing high-quality problem sets.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world, unregulated deployments.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, which are often too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to integrate explicit trajectory-level strategies into RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment during extended decision-making processes for AI agents.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new reinforcement learning approach.
RAO is designed to train recursive agents capable of spawning and delegating sub-tasks to themselves.
This recursive structure enables agents to scale to longer contexts and tackle complex problems using a divide-and-conquer strategy.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in LLM reasoning research: the lack of controlled, scalable environments for studying training scaling.
Introduces ScaleLogic, a synthetic framework designed to independently control two critical axes of difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Focuses on using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to systematically improve long-horizon reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a novel framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in complex collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the difficulty of jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents.

stories.title: Google broke reCAPTCHA for de-googled Android users
stories.author: anonymousiam
bullet_points: Google's reCAPTCHA system has been compromised or bypassed for users who have removed Google services from their Android devices (de-googled users).
The story touches upon the broader topic of Google Cloud fraud defense, suggesting potential evolution or alternatives to current CAPTCHA methods.
The discussion includes tangential commentary suggesting that Google Cloud Fraud Defence might be a repackaging of existing solutions (WEI).

stories.title: How I Evaluated an AI Model on AWS Without Writing a Single Line of Training Code
stories.author: tidding
bullet_points: The article provides a practical guide on using Amazon Bedrock for AI model evaluation.
It focuses on a workflow that minimizes coding, specifically avoiding model training code.
The tutorial covers the end-to-end process, from S3 setup to interpreting evaluation results.
